option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"C:/Users/nicks/IdeaProjects/monopoly/Monopoly":"{\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/screens/MenuScreen.kt\":\"This file contains two Composable functions:\\n\\n`MenuScreen`: A composable function that creates the main menu screen for the Monopoly game, including a background image, a game title, a \\\"Play\\\" button, and a \\\"How to Play\\\" button that opens a rules dialog.\\n\\n`MonopolyRulesDialog`: A composable function that creates a dialog box displaying the rules of the Monopoly game, with a \\\"Got it!\\\" button to dismiss the dialog.\\n\\nThe file is responsible for the user interface of the Monopoly game\\u0027s main menu and rules dialog.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/enums/ActionType.kt\":\"This file defines an `enum` class called `ActionType` that represents the different types of actions that can be performed in a Monopoly game. The `enum` class contains the following values:\\n\\n`ActionType`: An `enum` class that represents the different types of actions that can be performed in a Monopoly game, such as paying rent, purchasing property, going to jail, paying the bank, buying and selling houses and hotels, mortgaging property, surrendering, getting out of jail, and skipping a turn.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/screens/TurnOrderScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`TurnOrderScreen`: This function displays a screen that shows the order in which players will take their turns in a Monopoly game, and allows the user to re-randomize the order and proceed to the next screen.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes, but it does define the following functions:\\n\\n`getNewShuffledOrder`: This function generates a new shuffled order of player tokens, ensuring that it is different from the current order.\\n\\nOverall, this file is responsible for rendering a screen that displays the turn order for a Monopoly game and provides functionality to re-randomize the order and move to the next screen.\",\"/composeApp/build/generated/compose/resourceGenerator/kotlin/commonResClass/monopoly/composeapp/generated/resources/Res.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin file that provides a utility class `Res` for accessing resources in a Compose application. The file does not contain any classes, but it does have several functions and objects:\\n\\n`readBytes`: Reads the content of a resource file at the specified path and returns it as a byte array.\\n\\n`getUri`: Returns the URI string of a resource file at the specified path.\\n\\n`drawable`: An object that likely contains references to drawable resources.\\n\\n`string`: An object that likely contains references to string resources.\\n\\n`array`: An object that likely contains references to array resources.\\n\\n`plurals`: An object that likely contains references to plural resources.\\n\\n`font`: An object that likely contains references to font resources.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a convenient way to access various types of resources in a Compose application, such as images, strings, and fonts, by abstracting away the details of resource management.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Property.kt\":\"`Property`: A class that represents a property in the Monopoly game, with properties such as name, price, color, ownership status, and rent calculation.\\n\\n`purchase`: Attempts to purchase the property for the given player if they have enough money and the property is not already purchased.\\n\\n`chargeRent`: Calculates and charges rent to a player landing on this property, based on the property\\u0027s type and development.\\n\\n`calculateRent`: Calculates the rent amount based on the property\\u0027s type and development.\\n\\n`calculateUtilityRent`: Calculates the rent for a utility property.\\n\\n`calculateRailroadRent`: Calculates the rent for a railroad property.\\n\\n`calculateHouseRent`: Calculates the rent for a property with houses.\\n\\n`calculateHotelRent`: Calculates the rent for a property with a hotel.\\n\\n`buildHouse`: Builds a house on the property if possible, given the owner has enough money.\\n\\n`buildHotel`: Builds a hotel on the property if possible, given the owner has enough money.\\n\\n`getHousePrice`: Gets the price to build a house on the property.\\n\\n`getHotelPrice`: Gets the price to build a hotel on the property.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/dashboard/GameBoardView.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin Compose UI component that represents the game board view for a Monopoly game. It does not contain any classes, but it does have the following functions:\\n\\n`GameBoardView`: This function creates the overall layout of the game board, including the top row, the central area, and the bottom row.\\n\\n`CellView`: This function creates a single cell view within the game board, with different properties based on whether it is a corner cell or a top/bottom cell.\\n\\n`CentralAreaView`: This function creates the central area of the game board, which likely contains additional UI elements related to the game state.\\n\\nThe file is responsible for rendering the visual representation of the Monopoly game board using Kotlin Compose, a declarative UI framework for Android.\",\"/composeApp/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle build script for a Kotlin Multiplatform project that uses the Jetbrains Compose Desktop framework to create a desktop application. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather configures the project\\u0027s dependencies, build settings, and packaging options for the desktop application.\\n\\nThe key elements of the file are:\\n\\n`plugins`: Applies the necessary Kotlin Multiplatform and Jetbrains Compose Desktop plugins.\\n`kotlin`: Configures the Kotlin Multiplatform source sets and dependencies for the desktop target.\\n`compose.desktop`: Configures the desktop application settings, including the main class, package name, and target distribution formats.\\n\\nOverall, this file sets up the build environment for a Kotlin Multiplatform desktop application using the Jetbrains Compose framework.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/App.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin code for a Monopoly game built using the Jetpack Compose framework. It does not contain any classes, but it does have several functions:\\n\\n`App()`: This is the main composable function that manages the different screens and states of the Monopoly game, including the menu, player selection, token selection, turn order, and the game board layout.\\n\\n`MenuScreen()`: This function displays the main menu screen, allowing the user to start a new game.\\n\\n`PlayerSelectionScreen()`: This function allows the user to select the number of players for the game.\\n\\n`TokenSelectionScreen()`: This function allows the players to select their game tokens.\\n\\n`TurnOrderScreen()`: This function determines the turn order for the players based on their selected tokens.\\n\\n`Layout()`: This function is responsible for rendering the game board layout.\\n\\nOverall, this file is the main entry point for the Monopoly game and manages the different screens and states of the game.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/main.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it sets up a Jetpack Compose application window with a title of \\\"Monopoly\\\" and a default size of 1150 x 750 dp, positioned at 100 x 100 dp on the screen. The `main()` function is the entry point of the application, and it calls the `application` function to create the window and run the `App()` function, which is likely defined elsewhere in the codebase.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Cell.kt\":\"This file defines a single class, `Cell`, which represents a cell on a Monopoly board. The class has several properties that describe the type of cell, such as whether it is a property, a chance card, or a tax square.\\n\\n`Cell`: Represents a cell on a Monopoly board, with properties that describe the type of cell.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Game.kt\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`Game`: A class that represents a game of Monopoly, with properties for the player\\u0027s position, whether they owe the bank or another player, and the date of the game.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a data class to represent a Monopoly game.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/property/PropertyListView.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`PropertyListView`: This function creates a UI layout for displaying a list of properties, with separate columns for the selected player\\u0027s properties.\\n\\nThe file appears to be part of a Monopoly game application built using the Jetpack Compose framework. It defines a UI component that displays a list of properties, likely to be used in the game\\u0027s property management or ownership screen.\",\"/composeApp/build/generated/compose/resourceGenerator/kotlin/desktopMainResourceCollectors/monopoly/composeapp/generated/resources/ActualResourceCollectors.kt\":\"This file is a Kotlin file that defines a set of resources for a Monopoly Compose app. It does not contain any classes, but it defines several functions that provide access to various types of resources, including drawable resources, string resources, string array resources, plural string resources, and font resources.\\n\\n`Res.allDrawableResources`: Provides a map of all drawable resources used in the app.\\n`Res.allStringResources`: Provides a map of all string resources used in the app.\\n`Res.allStringArrayResources`: Provides a map of all string array resources used in the app.\\n`Res.allPluralStringResources`: Provides a map of all plural string resources used in the app.\\n`Res.allFontResources`: Provides a map of all font resources used in the app.\\n\\nThe file also includes a function `_collectCommonMainDrawable0Resources` that is used to collect the drawable resources, but this function is not documented in the provided code.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/utils/Utils.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It appears to be an empty package file for the `com.dallascollege.monopoly.utils` package, which likely serves as a container for utility classes or functions related to the Monopoly game application.\",\"/composeApp/build/generated/compose/resourceGenerator/kotlin/commonMainResourceCollectors/monopoly/composeapp/generated/resources/ExpectResourceCollectors.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines several internal `expect` properties that provide access to various types of resources, such as drawable resources, string resources, string array resources, plural string resources, and font resources. These properties are marked as `@ExperimentalResourceApi`, indicating that they are part of an experimental API and may change in the future.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a way to access different types of resources used in the Monopoly Compose App, which is likely a Kotlin Multiplatform project that uses the Compose UI framework.\",\"/.fleet/receipt.json\":\"This file appears to be a JSON configuration file for a Kotlin Multiplatform project. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather specifies the project\\u0027s configuration, including the template ID, target platforms, and UI frameworks to be used.\\n\\nThe key elements of the file are:\\n\\n`\\\"spec\\\"`: Defines the project specification, including the template ID and the target platforms and UI frameworks.\\n`\\\"template_id\\\"`: Specifies the template ID for the project, which is \\\"kmt\\\" (Kotlin Multiplatform).\\n`\\\"targets\\\"`: Defines the target platforms for the project, which in this case is \\\"desktop\\\" with the \\\"compose\\\" UI framework.\\n`\\\"timestamp\\\"`: Provides the timestamp when the project was generated, in this case, \\\"2025-02-20T06:15:33.914763726Z\\\".\\n\\nOverall, this file sets up the configuration for a Kotlin Multiplatform project targeting the desktop platform with the Compose UI framework.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/layout/Layout.kt\":\"This file defines the layout of the Monopoly game UI using Jetpack Compose. It consists of a main `Layout` function that arranges the game board, player list, action area, property list, and dice roller in a responsive layout.\\n\\n`Layout`: Arranges the game board, player list, action area, property list, and dice roller in a responsive layout.\\n\\n`GameBoardView`: Displays the game board.\\n\\n`PlayersListView`: Displays the list of players.\\n\\n`ActionArea`: Displays the action area for the selected player.\\n\\n`PropertyListView`: Displays the list of properties.\\n\\n`DiceRoller`: Displays the dice roller.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/logic/GameEngine.kt\":\"`GameEngine`: A class that represents the game engine for the Monopoly game, handling the game logic and player actions.\\n\\n`rollDice`: A function that generates a random dice roll (sum of two 6-sided dice) and returns the result.\\n\\n`nextTurn`: A function that handles the next turn of the game, including rolling the dice, moving the player, and printing the result.\\n\\nThis file defines the `GameEngine` class, which is responsible for managing the game logic and player actions in the Monopoly game. It includes functions for rolling the dice and handling the next turn of the game.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/PlayerPropertyManagement.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains a collection of utility functions for managing various aspects of a Monopoly game, such as property purchases, rent calculations, bankruptcy handling, and property trading. Here are the functions in the file:\\n\\n`askPlayerIfTheyWantToBuy`: Asks the player if they want to buy a property.\\n`notifyPropertyPurchased`: Notifies that a property was purchased.\\n`notifyInsufficientFunds`: Notifies that a player has insufficient funds.\\n`notifyRentPaid`: Notifies that rent was paid.\\n`buildHouse`: Builds a house on a property if possible.\\n`buildHotel`: Builds a hotel on a property if possible.\\n`mortgageProperty`: Mortgages a property if possible.\\n`liftMortgage`: Lifts the mortgage on a property if possible.\\n`calculateRent`: Calculates the rent for a property.\\n`tradeProperties`: Trades properties between two players.\\n`auctionProperty`: Auctions a property to the highest bidder.\\n`handleBankruptcy`: Handles a player\\u0027s bankruptcy.\\n`checkMonopoly`: Checks if a player has a monopoly on a color group.\\n`calculateUtilityRent`: Calculates the rent for a utility property.\\n`calculateRailroadRent`: Calculates the rent for a railroad property.\\n`transferProperty`: Transfers a property to a new owner.\\n`checkPropertyStatus`: Checks the status of a property.\\n`sellProperty`: Sells a property.\\n`canImproveProperty`: Checks if a player can improve a property.\\n`removeImprovement`: Removes an improvement from a property.\\n`notifyPropertySet`: Notifies when a player has a monopoly on a color group.\\n`calculatePropertySetRent`: Calculates the rent for a property with a monopoly.\\n`notifyPropertySetRent`: Notifies when a player\\u0027s monopoly doubles the rent.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/GameBoard.kt\":\"`GameBoard`: A class that represents the game board for the Monopoly game, including players, turn order, current turn, selected player, central money, speed die mode, free parking rule, cells, and properties.\\n\\n`createCells()`: A private function that creates an array of `Cell` objects representing the cells on the game board.\\n\\n`createProperties()`: A private function that creates an array of `Property` objects representing the properties on the game board.\\n\\n`createModels()`: A function that calls `createProperties()` and `createCells()` to initialize the game board.\\n\\nThis file defines the `GameBoard` class, which is responsible for managing the game board and its various components, such as players, cells, and properties. The class also includes functions to create the cells and properties that make up the game board.\",\"/settings.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle build script that configures the settings for a Gradle-based project named \\\"Monopoly\\\". It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather sets up the project\\u0027s dependency resolution and plugin management. Here\\u0027s a summary of the file\\u0027s contents:\\n\\n`rootProject.name`: Sets the name of the root project to \\\"Monopoly\\\".\\n\\n`enableFeaturePreview(\\\"TYPESAFE_PROJECT_ACCESSORS\\\")`: Enables a Gradle feature preview for type-safe project accessors.\\n\\n`pluginManagement`: Configures the repositories to be used for resolving Gradle plugins, including Google\\u0027s Maven repository and Maven Central.\\n\\n`dependencyResolutionManagement`: Configures the repositories to be used for resolving project dependencies, including Google\\u0027s Maven repository and Maven Central.\\n\\n`include(\\\":composeApp\\\")`: Includes the \\\":composeApp\\\" subproject in the overall project.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/enums/Token.kt\":\"This file defines an enumeration class and a function for converting the enumeration values to image strings.\\n\\n`Token`: An enumeration class that represents the different game tokens in the Monopoly game.\\n\\n`convertTokenToImageStr`: A function that takes a `Token` enumeration value and returns the corresponding image string representation.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/dice/DiceRoller.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function `DiceRoller` that represents a UI component for a dice roller in a Monopoly game.\\n\\n`DiceRoller`: A Composable function that displays a dice roller UI, allowing the user to roll two dice and update the game board accordingly.\\n\\n`rollDice`: A function that rolls the two dice, updates the current player\\u0027s position on the game board, and updates the UI to display the new dice values.\\n\\n`DiceView`: A Composable function that displays a single dice value.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/player/PlayerListView.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`PlayersListView`: This function displays a list of players in a Monopoly game, with each player\\u0027s information displayed in a separate view.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes. It is a Compose UI component that renders a list of players in a Monopoly game, with each player\\u0027s information displayed in a separate view.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Action.kt\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`Action`: A class that represents an action in the Monopoly game, with properties such as name, description, action type, property ID, money, and quantity.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It simply defines the `Action` class and its properties.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/dice/DiceView.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`DiceView`: This function creates a UI component that displays a dice value as a centered text within a rounded box.\\n\\nThe file appears to be a part of a Monopoly game application built using the Jetpack Compose framework for Android. It defines a reusable UI component that can be used to display the value of a dice roll in the game\\u0027s user interface.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/player/PlayerView.kt\":\"This file contains a Composable function `PlayerView` that displays a player\\u0027s information in a Compose UI.\\n\\n`PlayerView`: Displays a player\\u0027s information, including their token image and name, in a button that can be clicked to select the player.\\n\\n`selectPlayer`: Updates the `selectedPlayerId` property of the `GameBoard` object when the player\\u0027s button is clicked.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/action/ActionView.kt\":\"This file contains a Composable function `ActionView` that represents a UI component for an action view in a Monopoly game.\\n\\n`ActionView`: A Composable function that displays an action view for a Monopoly game, allowing the user to select an action type, quantity, and money, as well as a property to perform the action on, and provides a button to execute the selected action.\\n\\n`executeAction`: A function that is called when the \\\"Execute action\\\" button is clicked, but its implementation is not provided in the given code.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/DesktopApp.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It appears to be an empty package declaration for the `com.dallascollege.monopoly` package.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/dashboard/CellView.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`CellView`: This function renders a cell view for the Monopoly game board, displaying an image of the cell and allowing for the possibility of displaying player tokens within the cell.\\n\\nThe file appears to be part of the user interface for the Monopoly game, specifically the dashboard or game board view. It uses Jetpack Compose to create the cell view, which can be customized with various parameters such as whether it is a corner cell, the index of the cell, and whether it is on the top or bottom of the board.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/screens/TokenSelectionScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`TokenSelectionScreen`: This function creates a screen where players can select their tokens for a Monopoly game, allowing them to cycle through available tokens and select one for each player.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes. It is a Jetpack Compose UI screen that handles the token selection process for a Monopoly game.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/enums/PropertyColor.kt\":\"This file contains a single `enum` class:\\n\\n`PropertyColor`: An enumeration that represents the different property colors in the Monopoly game, along with the number of properties in each color group.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It serves as a centralized location to define the property color groups and the number of properties in each group, which is likely used throughout the Monopoly game application.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/dashboard/CentralAreaView.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`CentralAreaView`: This function renders a central area view for a Monopoly game board, displaying an image of the central area.\\n\\nThe file appears to be part of the user interface for a Monopoly game built using the Jetpack Compose framework. It defines a single composable function that displays an image of the central area of the game board, filling the available space.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/property/PropertyView.kt\":\"This file appears to be empty and does not contain any classes or functions. The file is likely a placeholder for a future implementation of the `com.dallascollege.monopoly.ui.property` package, which may contain classes and functions related to the user interface for property-related functionality in a Monopoly game.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Dice.kt\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`Dice`: A class that represents a single dice used in the Monopoly game.\\n\\nThe file also contains a single function:\\n\\n`roll()`: A function that simulates rolling a dice and returns a random integer between 1 and 6.\",\"/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a Gradle configuration file that sets up the necessary plugins for a Kotlin Multiplatform project with Compose Multiplatform. The purpose of this file is to ensure that the specified plugins are not loaded multiple times in each subproject\\u0027s classloader, which can cause issues. The plugins being used are:\\n\\n`libs.plugins.composeMultiplatform`: Applies the Compose Multiplatform plugin.\\n`libs.plugins.composeCompiler`: Applies the Compose Compiler plugin.\\n`libs.plugins.kotlinMultiplatform`: Applies the Kotlin Multiplatform plugin.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/action/ActionArea.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function `ActionArea` that renders the action area of a Monopoly game board. It consists of a header and a main content area that displays the `ActionView` component.\\n\\n`ActionArea`: Renders the action area of a Monopoly game board, including a header and the `ActionView` component.\",\"/composeApp/build/generated/compose/resourceGenerator/kotlin/commonMainResourceAccessors/monopoly/composeapp/generated/resources/Drawable0.commonMain.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin file that defines a set of resources for a Compose Multiplatform application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines a set of drawable resources that can be used in the application.\\n\\nThe file performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Defines a private object `CommonMainDrawable0` that contains a lazy-loaded `DrawableResource` for the \\\"compose_multiplatform\\\" resource.\\n2. Provides a function `_collectCommonMainDrawable0Resources` that collects the \\\"compose_multiplatform\\\" resource and adds it to a provided `MutableMap`.\\n3. Defines a property `Res.drawable.compose_multiplatform` that provides access to the \\\"compose_multiplatform\\\" `DrawableResource`.\\n4. Defines a private function `init_compose_multiplatform` that initializes the \\\"compose_multiplatform\\\" `DrawableResource`.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/Platform.kt\":\"This file defines a single class and a single function:\\n\\n`JVMPlatform`: A class that represents the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) platform, with a property that returns the version of Java being used.\\n\\n`getPlatform()`: A function that returns an instance of the `JVMPlatform` class.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Player.kt\":\"`Player`: A class that represents a player in the Monopoly game, with properties such as money, token, and a list of properties owned.\\n\\n`addProperty`: Adds a property to the player\\u0027s list of properties.\\n\\n`getRailroadCount`: Returns the number of railroad properties owned by the player.\\n\\n`getProperties`: Returns a list of all properties owned by the player.\\n\\n`canBuildHouse`: Checks if the player can build a house on a given property based on the number of properties they own in the same color group.\\n\\n`move`: Moves the player\\u0027s token on the game board by the specified number of steps.\\n\\n`getColorGroupSize`: Returns the size of the color group for a given property color.\\n\\n`addMoney`: Adds the specified amount of money to the player\\u0027s balance.\\n\\n`deductMoney`: Deducts the specified amount of money from the player\\u0027s balance.\\n\\n`getUtilityCount`: Returns the number of utility properties owned by the player.\\n\\n`getUtilityCount` (companion object): A static function that returns the number of utility properties owned by a given player.\",\"/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a description of a Kotlin Multiplatform project targeting Desktop, with a focus on the structure of the `/composeApp` folder. The key points are:\\n\\n- The `/composeApp` folder contains code that will be shared across Compose Multiplatform applications.\\n- The `commonMain` subfolder is for code that is common across all targets.\\n- Other subfolders (e.g., `iosMain`) are for Kotlin code that will be compiled for only the platform indicated in the folder name.\\n- The file provides a link to learn more about Kotlin Multiplatform.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/screens/PlayerSelectionScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`PlayerSelectionScreen`: This function creates a screen that allows the user to select the number of players for a Monopoly game, and then calls the `onPlayerSelected` callback function with the selected number of players.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes. It is a Jetpack Compose UI screen that allows the user to select the number of players for a Monopoly game and then proceed to the next screen.\"}"}
